summary:
The patient has negative HBsAg and absent markers of active HBV infection, making chronic or active Hepatitis B unlikely despite positive anti-HBc IgG. With documented anti-HCV antibodies and ultrasound findings consistent with cirrhosis, chronic hepatitis C is likely, necessitating confirmation of active viremia via HCV RNA PCR before starting directed antiviral therapy.  

Final Answer: $\boxed{\text{C}}$
